title: bupleurum commutatum subsp. glaucocarpum
intro: Bupleurum commutatum subsp. glaucocarpum, also known as the Chinese bockingkong or Bupleurum, is a plant species within the carrot family (Apiaceae) native to China. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiviral, and immunomodulatory effects. Its health benefits include reducing fever, alleviating stress and anxiety, and improving immune function. Bupleurum is often prepared as a tea, capsule, or tincture, and is traditionally used in Chinese medicine to treat various ailments, including respiratory infections and gastrointestinal issues.
